title: Steamed Whole Fish Cantonese

seo.title: Cantonese Steamed Fish Recipe | Authentic & Easy
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of authentic Cantonese Steamed Fish with our easy-to-follow recipe. Learn how to steam a whole fish to perfection with ginger, scallions, and soy sauce.
longTailKeywords: how to make Cantonese Steamed Fish
steamed fish with ginger and scallions recipe
lowCompetitionKeywords: steamed whole fish with soy sauce recipe
seo.imageAlt: A beautifully presented whole Cantonese Steamed Fish garnished with ginger, scallions, and cilantro.
seo.imageCaption: Experience the delicate flavors of a traditional Cantonese Steamed Fish, a classic Chinese seafood dish.

description: Experience the delicate flavors of a traditional Cantonese Steamed Fish, a culinary masterpiece known for its freshness and simplicity. This iconic dish features a whole fish perfectly steamed with aromatic ginger and scallions, then bathed in a savory soy-based sauce and hot oil. It's a healthy, elegant, and surprisingly easy recipe that highlights the natural sweetness of the fish.

ingredients.notes: half cut into 2-inch sections, half julienned for garnish

instructions.title: Prepare the Fish
instructions.text: Rinse the whole fish thoroughly, pat dry with paper towels. Make two shallow diagonal slits on each side of the fish. Rub the fish all over with salt and white pepper, and optionally, a splash of Shaoxing wine. Place some ginger slices and scallion sections inside the fish cavity and on top.

instructions.title: Set Up Steamer
instructions.text: Fill a large wok or steamer pot with water, ensuring it does not touch the steaming rack. Bring the water to a rolling boil over high heat.

instructions.title: Steam the Fish
instructions.text: Carefully place the fish on a heatproof plate or shallow dish that fits inside your steamer. Transfer the plate to the steamer rack, cover tightly, and steam for 8-12 minutes, depending on the thickness of the fish. The fish is done when the flesh is opaque and flakes easily.

instructions.title: Prepare the Sauce
instructions.text: While the fish is steaming, combine light soy sauce, sesame oil, sugar, and water or chicken broth in a small bowl. Stir until the sugar dissolves.

instructions.title: Assemble and Garnish
instructions.text: Once the fish is cooked, carefully remove the plate from the steamer. Discard the ginger slices and scallion sections used during steaming. Pour off any excess liquid accumulated on the plate. Arrange the fresh julienned ginger, julienned scallions, and chopped cilantro evenly over the steamed fish.

instructions.title: Hot Oil Drizzle
instructions.text: Heat the peanut oil or vegetable oil in a small saucepan until it is smoking hot. Carefully pour the hot oil directly over the julienned ginger and scallions on the fish. You should hear a sizzling sound, which helps release their aromatic flavors.

instructions.title: Serve Immediately
instructions.text: Immediately pour the prepared soy sauce mixture over the fish. Serve hot with steamed white rice for a complete and delicious meal.

tips: Freshness is Key: Always choose the freshest whole fish available for the best flavor and texture. Look for clear eyes, red gills, and firm flesh.
Proper Steaming: Do not overcook the fish. The steaming time can vary based on the fish's thickness. A good rule of thumb is 8-10 minutes per inch of thickness at its thickest point.
Sizzling Oil: The hot oil drizzle is crucial. It 'cooks' the fresh aromatics (ginger and scallions) directly on the fish, releasing their fragrant oils and adding an irresistible sizzle.
article: Steamed Whole Fish Cantonese is a beloved Chinese Seafood dish enjoyed across Chinese. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.

recipeIngredient: 1.5 - 2 lbs Whole fish (snapper, branzino, or rockfish)
3 tablespoons Peanut oil or vegetable oil
